summary: FPV drones are being used in increasingly creative ways, including attempts to disrupt Russian forces on horseback before striking, and a reported first where a Russian soldier charged at a Ukrainian FPV drone with a bayonet and was killed. Russian operators from the Rubikon Center claim 104 Ukrainian targets destroyed on March 11, 2026, including personnel and fixed-wing UAVs intercepted by the "Yolka" air defense drone using kinetic means. Ukrainian forces are also deploying FPV detectors and increasing use of "Molniya" strike and reconnaissance drones, though these are reportedly difficult to counter due to unknown control frequencies. On the ground, a Ukrainian MaxxPro armored vehicle was destroyed by a Russian fiber-optic FPV drone "Barmaley" in Dobropole, and Russian soldiers are being hit by multiple simultaneous FPV strikes.
